purpose: Reassign one account to a different group within one financialList. Used by the Trial Balance tab's grouping column.

read_tb_grouped.purpose: Returns the TB rows annotated with each account's current group in the specified list. Read this before a PATCH.

patch.purpose: Reassign one account to a different group.
patch.bulk: scripts.groups.build_bulk_assign_js(client_id, patch_bodies, headers) - one browser call, N PATCHes, order-independent
patch.verification_status: live-verified 2026-05-30 (clientId 100173): PATCH WORKS and sticks. The earlier 'silent no-op' warning was a FALSE ALARM caused by a read-back bug - the new group reads back at row.account.group, NOT top-level row.group.

patch_subgroup.purpose: Assign one account to a SUB-group (child of a group).

request_body_shape_patch.kind: full account row with `group` field replaced

group_subobject.financialGroupType: string ('Custom' for user-defined groups, 'Global' for CCH-bundled groups like FS Financial defaults)
group_subobject.name: string

request_body_shape_patch.note: The wire body sent by the UI carries ~30 fields. scripts.groups.assign_account_to_group preserves whatever row you read via GET and just swaps the group; build_patch_body() is the minimal hand-rolled version.

semantics.no_unassign_state: Every account is always in SOME group per list. There is no null group. To 'remove' from a custom group, reassign to whatever Global group fits.
semantics.autosaves_immediately: Every reassignment fires its own PATCH; no batch save button on the TB tab. Brett's note: 'CCH was watching the whole time, doesn't let you save when you're happy, you save every change you make on this one.'
semantics.incremental_per_account: Each PATCH touches one account. Bulk reassignment = N PATCHes (use build_bulk_assign_js for one browser call).
semantics.financialListId_required: GUARDRAIL: every regroup PATCH MUST carry financialListId. Omitting it -> HTTP 400 'FinancialListId ... request data invalid'; it does NOT fall back to the default list. Regroups are per-list independent (one account = one group PER list), so writing list X cannot touch list Y. Only real risk is passing the WRONG list id - before a bulk regroup, resolve the target list and assert it is not isDefault unless intended.
semantics.read_back_path: Verify an assignment at row.account.group.financialGroupId on the grouped TB (NOT top-level row.group).

open_follow_ups: Test whether a stripped-down PATCH body (just id + group) works - might save bandwidth on bulk ops
